text.title: Niche Viability Scoring
text.summary: 5-criteria weighted scoring model for comparing niche opportunities with decision thresholds.

rule.statement: Score 3-5 niches in parallel, never one in isolation. The framework produces a comparative ranking; its value is relative, not absolute.
rule.rationale: A single niche score of 7.3 is meaningless without a comparison set. Scoring in isolation produces a number that confirms existing bias rather than forcing a real trade-off decision.

section.title: Five Criteria (fixed weights for indie SaaS)

list.item: Market Size (25%): score 1-10 where 1-3 = below $10M, 4-6 = $10-100M, 7-10 = above $100M SAM
Competition (20%): score 1-10 where 1-3 = red ocean (10+ direct competitors), 4-6 = moderate (3-10), 7-10 = blue ocean (fewer than 3)
Barriers to Entry (20%): score 1-10 where 1-3 = high barriers (capital, regulatory, patents), 4-6 = medium (technical expertise), 7-10 = low (execution only)
Profitability (20%): score 1-10 where 1-3 = thin margins below 20%, 4-6 = OK margins 20-40%, 7-10 = high margins above 40%
Your Fit (15%): score 1-10 where 1-3 = no relevant skills or audience, 4-6 = some transferable skills, 7-10 = deep expertise and existing audience

rule.statement: Cite a source URL for each criterion score. A score without a link is noise an agent can fabricate.
rule.rationale: Unanchored scores shift with the analyst's mood across sessions; URL-backed scores are reproducible and challengeable.

list.item: 5.5-7.4: Proceed with caution — identify the weakest criterion and mitigate
3.5-5.4: Significant risks — require additional validation before committing
below 3.5: Pass — do not enter this niche

section.title: Weight Adjustment Warning

rule.statement: Keep criteria weights under version control. If weights are adjusted mid-comparison, restart the entire batch from scratch.
rule.rationale: Changing weights after some niches are scored invalidates cross-niche comparisons; partial weight changes produce scores that cannot be compared against each other.

section.note: The 25/20/20/20/15 weights were tuned for indie SaaS. Enterprise sales, hardware, and marketplace models need different weightings — document the change before scoring.

example.code:
## Niche: AI Writing Tool
| Criterion | Score | Weight | Weighted | Source | |-------------|-------|--------|----------|-------------------------------| | Market Size | 8 | 25% | 2.00 | statista.com/ai-writing-2025 | | Competition | 4 | 20% | 0.80 | g2.com/categories/ai-writing | | Barriers | 6 | 20% | 1.20 | openai.com/pricing | | Profitability| 7 | 20% | 1.40 | baremetrics.com/open | | Your Fit | 8 | 15% | 1.20 | own assessment, ML background | | **Total** | | | **6.60** | |
Decision: PROCEED WITH CAUTION — differentiation required vs. crowded field Risk: Competition score 4; mitigation: narrow to [specific vertical]
example.note: One score at 4 (below 5) and one at 8 (above 7) — avoids default scoring of 6/7 for everything.

text.reference: Niche viability scorecard template with justification column
Script that validates weight sum and recomputes total from JSON sidecar
